pattern_recognition: Binary Search, because the array is sorted. Signals include the need to find specific indices rather than just presence, which suggests a search strategy.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check each element in the array to find the first and last position of the target.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through the array, keeping track of the first and last indices where the target is found.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use binary search to efficiently find the first and last positions of the target in O(log n) time.
approaches.algorithm: Perform binary search to find the first occurrence, then perform another binary search to find the last occurrence.

dry_run: Given the input array [5, 7, 7, 8, 8, 10] and target 8, the first binary search will find the index of the first 8 at position 3. The second binary search will find the index of the last 8 at position 4. The output will be [3, 4].
edge_cases: Empty input array should return [-1, -1]. If the array has only one element, it should return [0, 0] if that element matches the target, otherwise [-1, -1]. Duplicates should be handled correctly to ensure both first and last positions are found. Large input sizes should be tested for performance.
common_pitfalls: Common mistakes include: 1) Not handling the case where the target is not present, leading to incorrect indices. 2) Failing to consider duplicates, which can result in incorrect last index. 3) Using linear search instead of binary search for sorted arrays, leading to inefficient solutions.
